title: Information-Aware KV Cache Compression for Long Reasoning
abstract: arXiv:2606.26875v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Reasoning capability has advanced rapidly in large language models (LLMs), leading to an increasing size of key-value (KV) cache in both prefilling and decoding stages. Existing KV cache compression methods mainly rely on attention weights to estimate token importance. While attention effectively captures contextual relevance, it overlooks complementary information-theoretic signals related to predictive uncertainty and token informativeness. In this paper, we revisit token importance from a forward-looking perspective and introduce \textit{Forward Influence}, a metric that measures how compressed tokens affect future contexts. Our analysis reveals that tokens selected by attention scores mainly influence nearby contexts, whereas tokens associated with high predictive uncertainty exhibit substantially stronger influence on distant future contexts. Based on the observation, we propose \textbf{InfoKV}, an entropy-aware KV cache compressi

Abstract: arXiv:2606.26875v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Reasoning capability has advanced rapidly in large language models (LLMs), leading to an increasing size of key-value (KV) cache in both prefilling and decoding stages. Existing KV cache compression methods mainly rely on attention weights to estimate token importance. translated_title: 面向长推理场景的信息感知型KV缓存压缩方法
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26875v1 公告类型：交叉摘要。推理能力在大语言模型（LLMs）中快速进步，导致在预填充和解码阶段中键值（KV）缓存的大小不断增加。现有的KV缓存压缩方法主要依赖注意力权重来估计令牌重要性。虽然注意力能有效捕捉上下文相关性，但它忽略了与预测不确定性和令牌信息量相关的互补信息论信号。本文从前瞻性视角重新审视令牌重要性，并引入\textit{前向影响}这一指标，用于衡量被压缩的令牌如何影响未来上下文。我们的分析揭示：由注意力分数选出的令牌主要影响邻近上下文，而与高预测不确定性相关的令牌对未来远距离上下文表现出显著更强的影响。基于这一观察，我们提出\textbf{InfoKV}，一种熵感知的KV缓存压缩方法。
tldr: 本文提出InfoKV方法，通过引入“前向影响”指标来利用预测不确定性信号，实现比传统注意力权重更优的KV缓存压缩，尤其能更好地保留对远距离未来上下文有重要影响的令牌。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes InfoKV, which leverages predictive uncertainty via a "Forward Influence" metric for KV cache compression, outperforming attention-based methods by better preserving tokens critical for distant future contexts.
